abstract: A key whose value indicates the item’s application label.
overview:
## Discussion

The corresponding value is of type [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFData] and contains a label for this item. This attribute is different from the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrLabel] attribute, which is intended to be human-readable. Instead, this attribute is used to look up a key programmatically; in particular, for keys of class [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrKeyClassPublic] and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrKeyClassPrivate], the value of this attribute is the hash of the public key.

# kSecAttrApplicationLabel

**Global Variable**

A key whose value indicates the item’s application label.

## Declaration

```swift
let kSecAttrApplicationLabel: CFString
```

## Discussion

The corresponding value is of type [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFData] and contains a label for this item. This attribute is different from the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrLabel] attribute, which is intended to be human-readable. Instead, this attribute is used to look up a key programmatically; in particular, for keys of class [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrKeyClassPublic] and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecAttrKeyClassPrivate], the value of this attribute is the hash of the public key.
title: kSecAttrApplicationLabel
